input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-24T00:53:55.808Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated zero tests as a pass.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

data.content:
Exit code 8 means "no tests discovered" — a new behavior in Microsoft.Testing.Platform. VSTest silently returned 0 in that case.

The fix is one MSBuild property:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code 8 when zero tests are discovered. The fix is to set the `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` MSBuild property to `0` in the project file, which tells the platform to return exit code 0 when no tests are found.

Let me add that property.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-208303860e004c059a8396de18cb45ec/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-208303860e004c059a8396de18cb45ec/TestProject.csproj b/tmp/sv-208303860e004c059a8396de18cb45ec/TestProject.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-208303860e004c059a8396de18cb45ec/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-208303860e004c059a8396de18cb45ec/TestProject.csproj
@@ -4,6 +4,8 @@
     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
+    <!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered -->
+    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />

data.content: The correct MSBuild property is `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments`. Let me fix the `.csproj`:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat "zero tests discovered" (exit code 8) as success -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**What happened:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (unlike VSTest which returned 0). This causes `dotnet test` to report failure in CI.

**The fix:** Added `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` with `--ignore-exit-code 8` to the `.csproj`. This tells the test platform to treat "no tests found" as success, restoring VSTest behavior.

Alternatively, you can pass it at the command line without modifying the project:
```
dotnet test -- --ignore-exit-code 8
```
